data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced a high error rate due to repeated Redis connection timeouts, impacting user session management.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The qa-login service faced numerous 'Session store retrieval failure' errors due to Redis connection timeouts occurring consistently after 5000ms.

evidenceChain: High error rate detected with a consistent message: 'LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.'
Prometheus query results show a significant proportion of HTTP 500 errors.

timeline.event: Session store retrieval failure due to Redis connection timeout logged.
Session store retrieval failure due to Redis connection timeout logged.
Session store retrieval failure due to Redis connection timeout logged.
Session store retrieval failure due to Redis connection timeout logged.

data.impact: Users experienced session-related issues, likely causing service unavailability or degraded login functionality.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the Redis instance serving qa-login to clear any orphaned connections.
Evaluate and optimize the timeout settings for Redis in qa-login to prevent similar issues.
Implement enhanced monitoring of Redis latency and connection status.
